title: Pungent Pale Turkish Epoisses Pilaf
description: A briny and shattering Turkish dish featuring epoisses cheese, served in a scallop shell, perfect for a brunch mood. This Mediterranean-friendly pilaf is a unique and flavorful experience.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the bulgur according to package instructions.
While the bulgur is cooking, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the artichoke hearts, olives, and lemon juice to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes, until heated through.
Flake the epoisses cheese into small pieces and add to the skillet, stirring until melted and well combined.
Drain the cooked bulgur and add to the skillet, stirring to combine with the epoisses mixture.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Spoon the pilaf into scallop shells and garnish with chopped parsley.
Chill the pilaf in an ice bath for 10 minutes, until cooled and set.
Serve the pilaf immediately, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
